integrations.dyndbg.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/debug/dynamic_debug/control. Surfaces forgotten dyndbg= boot args or runtime echoes that leak kernel log volume and burn CPU on hot paths.
integrations.extcon.title: 🔗 USB-C / dock cable state
integrations.extcon.desc: Walks /sys/class/extcon/extcon*/. Catches USB-C / DP-alt-mode / dock mux stuck cable states and mutually-exclusive connectors (HDMI + DP on same mux) asserted simultaneously.
integrations.unixsock.title: 🔌 Unix socket inventory
integrations.unixsock.desc: Reads /proc/net/unix and breaks down by namespace (named, abstract, unnamed). Predicts ENOBUFS / FD-exhaustion before sock_pool_audit's coarser total-count threshold fires.
integrations.schedfeat.title: 🏃 CFS features + tuning
integrations.schedfeat.desc: Walks /sys/kernel/debug/sched/{features,latency_ns,min_granularity_ns,wakeup_granularity_ns,migration_cost_ns}. Catches gamer-guide tweaks that survived reboot and cause mysterious frame-time / CUDA-launch jitter.
integrations.wol.title: 🔌 WoL + link-speed downshift
integrations.wol.desc: Reads /sys/class/net/*/{operstate,carrier,duplex,speed,device/power/wakeup}. Catches WoL silently armed on a NIC with no link (S3 power drain) and gigabit ports running at 100M half (worn cable / dirty SFP).
integrations.thunderbolt.title: ⚡ Thunderbolt / USB4 DMA
integrations.thunderbolt.desc: Walks /sys/bus/thunderbolt/devices/. Catches an eGPU/dock running security=none (pre-IOMMU DMA attack surface) or a device with authorized=0 that explains 'my dock doesn't show up'.
integrations.nvmestate.title: 💽 NVMe controller state
integrations.nvmestate.desc: Walks /sys/class/nvme/nvme*/. Catches a controller stuck in resetting/connecting for hours and firmware-rev drift across identical drives (one drive is mysteriously slower).
integrations.workqueue.title: 🧵 Workqueue cpumask / isolation
integrations.workqueue.desc: Walks /sys/devices/virtual/workqueue/* and cross-refs /sys/devices/system/cpu/isolated. Catches kernel workqueues silently flooding isolated cores — destroys p99 latency on RT/LLM setups.
integrations.usbauth.title: 🔒 USB per-hub authorization
integrations.usbauth.desc: Reads /sys/bus/usb/devices/usb*/{authorized_default,interface_authorized_default} and detects USBGuard. Catches hubs that auto-authorize every plugged device with no userland gate — your laptop is wide open to BadUSB at every USB-C port.
integrations.proclocks.title: 🔐 /proc/locks contention
integrations.proclocks.desc: Parses /proc/locks for BLOCKED waiters (->\u00a0prefix) and resolves the PIDs to comms via /proc/<pid>/comm. Catches mail spool / database long lock chains that explain mysterious tail-latency spikes.
integrations.smtcontrol.title: 🧠 SMT control vs vulnerabilities
integrations.smtcontrol.desc: Cross-refs /sys/devices/system/cpu/smt/control with /sys/devices/system/cpu/vulnerabilities/*. Catches SMT disabled while the kernel still reports SMT vulnerable (needs microcode update) AND SMT disabled for no security benefit (paying a 30-50% perf tax on a homelab).
integrations.irqskew.title: 🎯 IRQ affinity_hint drift
integrations.irqskew.desc: Compares /proc/irq/<N>/affinity_hint (driver's per-queue placement) against actual smp_affinity_list. Catches irqbalance silently overriding the driver's NUMA-aware queue layout — your 8-queue NIC's careful work is being undone.
integrations.userspacehard.title: 🛡️ Userspace hardening sysctls
integrations.userspacehard.desc: Reads /proc/sys/{kernel/randomize_va_space, fs/protected_*, fs/suid_dumpable}. Catches the embarrassing 'fresh distro left ASLR off' and SUID-dumpable misconfigs that silently turn crash dumps into credential leaks.
integrations.suspendsel.title: 💤 Suspend mode selectors
integrations.suspendsel.desc: Reads /sys/power/{state,mem_sleep,disk,pm_test}. Catches the 'laptop draining 4W in sleep because firmware only exposes s2idle' trap and the dev-leftover pm_test that makes suspend silently no-op.
integrations.iommureserved.title: 🔐 IOMMU group type + reserved regions
integrations.iommureserved.desc: Walks /sys/kernel/iommu_groups/*/{type,reserved_regions} and cross-refs /proc/cmdline. Catches GPU groups silently dropping to identity mapping after a kernel update, MSI region overlaps, and DMA-vs-DMA-FQ flush latency drift.
integrations.timermigration.title: ⏱️ timer_migration × nohz_full drift
integrations.timermigration.desc: Cross-refs /proc/sys/kernel/timer_migration with /sys/devices/system/cpu/{nohz_full,isolated} and cmdline rcu_nocbs=. Catches the realtime/audio/LLM-inference rig where the operator forgot to disable timer_migration after enabling nohz_full — explains 'I isolated CPUs and latency is still spiky'.
integrations.tcpcc.title: 🌐 TCP congestion control + Fast Open
integrations.tcpcc.desc: Reads /proc/sys/net/ipv4/{tcp_congestion_control,tcp_available_congestion_control,tcp_fastopen}. Catches the 'we shipped HF dataset downloads on cubic + 200ms RTT and never noticed bbr was compiled in' misconfig on a homelab uplink.
integrations.nslimits.title: 🧱 Namespace caps (max_*_namespaces)
integrations.nslimits.desc: Reads /proc/sys/user/max_{user,pid,net,mnt,ipc,uts,cgroup,time}_namespaces. Catches the 'podman/bwrap silently fails to start' headache when a distro shipped max_user_namespaces=0 after a hardening update.
integrations.sysvipc.title: 📦 SysV IPC limits (kernel.shm*)
integrations.sysvipc.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/{shmmax,shmall,shmmni,msgmni} + MemTotal. Catches 'CUDA-MPS daemon refuses new clients' and 'PostgreSQL won't start with shared_buffers=8GB' caused by stock shm ceilings on a memory-rich rig.
integrations.pcielink.title: 🔌 PCIe link drift (NVMe + peripherals)
integrations.pcielink.desc: Walks /sys/bus/pci/devices/* and flags NVMe controllers + non-GPU peripherals running below their max link speed/width. GPUs owned by PCIe width watcher ; this catches the 4TB NVMe dropped to Gen3 x2 from a shifted ribbon, or the 2.5G NIC silently at Gen1 x1.
integrations.resctrl.title: 🧮 resctrl L3/MBA partition
integrations.resctrl.desc: Walks /sys/fs/resctrl/ for Intel RDT / AMD QoS allocations. Catches a partitioned L3 cache or memory-bandwidth throttle silently capping tokens/sec — invisible to nvidia-smi. Typical desktop returns 'unknown' (resctrl not mounted).
integrations.netproto.title: 📡 /proc/net AF_PACKET + raw socket leaks
integrations.netproto.desc: Reads /proc/net/{packet,raw,raw6}. Catches a tcpdump you forgot to Ctrl-C three days ago still slurping packets and stealing softirq budget from your GPU NIC, or an abandoned ICMP-loop daemon leaking raw sockets.
integrations.cpufreqgovtune.title: 🎛️ cpufreq governor tunables
integrations.cpufreqgovtune.desc: Reads /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpufreq/policy*/scaling_governor + schedutil/rate_limit_us. Catches a stale 20ms schedutil rate_limit (older Ubuntu default) that lets the CPU lag GPU feed during sparse-batch inference, or ondemand/conservative active on a modern kernel.
integrations.pciedpc.title: 🛑 PCIe DPC containment
integrations.pciedpc.desc: Walks /sys/bus/pci/devices/*/dpc/. Catches a silent DPC quarantine event (NVMe vanishes mid-train) that AER counters miss because the device was contained before AER could log non-fatal, or DPC capable but disabled on a fault-prone board.
integrations.cgrouppids.title: 🧬 cgroup pids.max controller
integrations.cgrouppids.desc: Walks /sys/fs/cgroup/**/pids.{max,current,events}. Catches a systemd-user-slice or container hitting pids.max — manifests as 'VS Code can't spawn a new pty' or 'docker exec returns resource temporarily unavailable' with zero kmsg signal.
integrations.dmabufbuf.title: 🖼️ dma_buf per-exporter inventory
integrations.dmabufbuf.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/debug/dma_buf/bufinfo. Surfaces 'Firefox/Chromium WebGL leaked 4 GiB of GPU-shared buffers' — the silent killer behind RTX 3090 VRAM mysteriously full with no nvidia-smi process attribution. Requires root for debugfs.
integrations.nvmehmb.title: 💾 NVMe Host Memory Buffer
integrations.nvmehmb.desc: Reads /sys/class/nvme/nvme*/hmb + /sys/module/nvme/parameters/max_host_mem_size_mb. Explains 'random 4K read stutter on my new $80 DRAM-less NVMe' — kernel param nvme.max_host_mem_size_mb=0 (common in hardened distros) silently kills HMB and tanks IOPS by 5x.
integrations.vmstatreclaim.title: 📉 vmstat reclaim/compaction deltas
integrations.vmstatreclaim.desc: Tracks /proc/vmstat counters with persisted state at ~/.config/gpu-dashboard/vmstat_prev.json. Catches the '12-hour LLM session that died with one OOM at 4am' — the THP-fragmentation + direct-reclaim death-spiral that PSI smooths over but vmstat deltas expose. First run returns 'unknown' (baseline saved).
integrations.iommustrict.title: 🔐 IOMMU strict/lazy DMA mode
integrations.iommustrict.desc: Reads /sys/module/{iommu,intel_iommu,amd_iommu}/parameters/* + /proc/cmdline. Catches the 'I rebooted with iommu.strict=0 for VFIO perf and forgot' footgun that hides silent DMA corruption, and detects passthrough configs that defeat DMA isolation.
integrations.lockupwatchdog.title: ⏰ Kernel lockup detector
integrations.lockupwatchdog.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/{watchdog,nmi_watchdog,watchdog_thresh} + /sys/devices/system/cpu/nmi_watchdog. Catches the 'GPU hangs the system but dmesg has zero softlockup traces' mystery when the lockup detector was silently disabled by a tuning preset.
integrations.khugepaged.title: 🧩 khugepaged THP collapse pressure
integrations.khugepaged.desc: Delta-tracks /proc/vmstat thp_collapse_alloc{_failed} via ~/.local/state/gpu-dashboard/khugepaged_prev.json. Catches silent THP collapse storms during model-load that stall GPU feeders for 100s of ms even when overall THP looks 'enabled'. First run returns 'unknown' (baseline saved).
integrations.drmfdinfo.title: 🎮 DRM client VRAM attribution
integrations.drmfdinfo.desc: Walks /proc/*/fdinfo/* parsing drm-pdev / drm-memory-vram. Pinpoints which PID is hogging the 3090's VRAM when nvidia-smi shows 100% but a generic 'python' — turns 'GPU full' into 'PID 14523 holds 22 GiB VRAM'. Requires root or self-PID access.
integrations.pipemq.title: 📨 pipe / POSIX-mqueue / epoll limits
integrations.pipemq.desc: Reads /proc/sys/fs/{pipe-max-size, pipe-user-pages-*, mqueue/*, epoll/max_user_watches}. Catches CI agents silently losing POSIX mqueue messages and IDEs hitting epoll watch ceilings on big monorepos.
integrations.cgmempeak.title: 📈 cgroup v2 memory.peak ratio
integrations.cgmempeak.desc: Walks /sys/fs/cgroup/**/memory.{peak,max,high,swap.peak,events.local}. Surfaces the systemd-user-slice / Flatpak / Steam scope that quietly hit memory.high last night or used swap — without journalctl.
integrations.nfsmountstats.title: 🌐 NFS RPC transport health
integrations.nfsmountstats.desc: Parses /proc/self/mountstats xprt counters. Catches the homelab Jellyfin library mount silently retransmitting half its reads because the AP roamed — invisible to df/iostat.
integrations.bpfjit.title: ⚙️ BPF JIT + XDP + busy_poll
integrations.bpfjit.desc: Reads /proc/sys/net/core/{bpf_jit_enable, busy_poll} + /sys/class/net/*/xdp. Catches the rare-but-vicious case where eBPF gets installed on a box whose distro defaults have bpf_jit_enable=0 — every packet pays the interpreter tax.
integrations.hwpoison.title: 🧠 hwpoison / memory_failure
integrations.hwpoison.desc: Reads /proc/meminfo HardwareCorrupted + /proc/vmstat hwpoison_*. Catches silent DRAM bit-rot that has retired host pages but hasn't yet tipped EDAC thresholds — the 'why did my training segfault randomly' surprise.
integrations.aiofanotify.title: 🧵 fs.aio + fanotify limits
integrations.aiofanotify.desc: Reads /proc/sys/fs/{aio-max-nr, fanotify/*}. Catches aio-max-nr still at the kernel-default 64K on Docker/MinIO boxes and fanotify mark caps that would EBUSY antivirus/container engines.
integrations.ttmpool.title: 🖼️ DRM TTM page pool caps
integrations.ttmpool.desc: Reads /sys/module/ttm/parameters/{page_pool_size, pages_limit, dma32_pages_limit}. Flags when caps are at kernel-auto-tune defaults — the host RAM mirror of VRAM can quietly eat MemAvailable on VRAM-overcommit scenarios.
integrations.lockdep.title: 🔍 Kernel lockdep posture
integrations.lockdep.desc: Reads /proc/lockdep_stats. Catches the 'shipped a debug kernel' footgun (PROVE_LOCKING burning 5-10% CPU) and the silent lockdep death (debug_locks=0 after MAX_LOCKDEP_* hit) that hides every subsequent deadlock warning — a uniquely-homelab 'weird kernel from AUR' find.
integrations.mdiophy.title: 🌐 Ethernet PHY + EEE
integrations.mdiophy.desc: Reads /sys/class/net/*/phydev/{link,speed,duplex,eee/*}. Catches the silent reason your 10GbE NIC stays at 100Mbps after suspend — when carrier=1 but the PHY didn't actually re-establish autoneg.
integrations.modrefcnt.title: 📦 Kernel module refcnt + holders
integrations.modrefcnt.desc: Reads /sys/module/*/refcnt + initstate + holders/. Pinpoints why `modprobe -r nvidia` won't work — names the offending holder before you reboot.
integrations.tracingbuf.title: 🔍 ftrace ring-buffer footprint
integrations.tracingbuf.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/tracing/{buffer_*_size_kb, per_cpu/cpuN/stats, trace_clock}. Catches 8 GB ftrace ring-buffers left after a debug session quietly eating RAM your model needs, plus active event drops and bogus cross-CPU ordering.
integrations.pdwakeup.title: 💡 Per-device wakeup attribution
integrations.pdwakeup.desc: Walks /sys/devices/.../power/wakeup_*. Names the specific device (e.g. 0000:00:14.0 xHCI or i2c-ELAN9009) that just cost your homelab 12 W of overnight idle by aborting suspend.
integrations.discardcaps.title: ✂️ Block discard / TRIM caps
integrations.discardcaps.desc: Reads /sys/block/*/queue/{discard_max_bytes, discard_granularity} + SCSI provisioning_mode. Catches SSDs where the kernel says 'TRIM not supported' because of a SCSI provisioning_mode=full quirk — fstrim silently succeeds while reclaiming zero bytes.
integrations.cpusetv2.title: 🧮 cgroup v2 cpuset partition
integrations.cpusetv2.desc: Walks /sys/fs/cgroup/**/cpuset.{cpus, cpus.effective, cpus.partition}. Surfaces the 'my container's pinned to CPU 0-7 but CPU 0-7 got isolated at boot, so cpuset.cpus.effective is empty and the workload runs on whatever the parent allows' trap.
integrations.traceinst.title: 🔧 ftrace per-instance buffers
integrations.traceinst.desc: Walks /sys/kernel/tracing/instances/*. Finds the multi-GB ring buffer some bpftrace / trace-cmd / perfetto invocation left armed under instances/<name>/ after the user Ctrl-C'd — invisible to top-level checks.
integrations.blockstack.title: 🧱 dm/md block stack topology
integrations.blockstack.desc: Walks /sys/block/{dm-*,md*}/ + holders/slaves graph + /proc/{mounts,swaps}. Catches the 'I cryptsetup luksClose'd but the dm device is still there with no holders, pinning a 2TB LVM PE pool' homelab landmine, plus dm-thin pools left suspended after snapshot.
integrations.kvmmmu.title: 🧠 KVM MMU + NX-huge / EPT/NPT
integrations.kvmmmu.desc: Reads /sys/module/kvm/parameters/{tdp_mmu, nx_huge_pages, nx_huge_pages_recovery_ratio} + kvm_intel/ept + kvm_amd/npt. Surfaces the iTLB-multihit mitigation (nx_huge_pages) being silently off, EPT/NPT disabled (slow shadow paging), or the recovery ratio pinned to 0 (recovered pages never re-collapsed).
integrations.zfsarc.title: 🐟 ZFS ARC tuning
integrations.zfsarc.desc: Reads /proc/spl/kstat/zfs/arcstats + /sys/module/zfs/parameters/{zfs_arc_max, zfs_arc_min} + /proc/meminfo. Catches the unbounded ARC (zfs_arc_max=0 → grows to 50 % of RAM by default) that homelabs find out about when desktop apps OOM hours after a zpool import.
integrations.cgdelegate.title: 🪜 cgroup slice delegation + freeze
integrations.cgdelegate.desc: Walks /sys/fs/cgroup/{system.slice, user@<uid>.service}/cgroup.{subtree_control, freeze, events, procs} + /sys/kernel/cgroup/delegate. Catches frozen workloads, zombie sub-cgroups, and the systemd default of user-slice missing the io controller (no IO accounting for desktop services).
integrations.d3cold.title: 💤 GPU D3cold cascade + runtime PM
integrations.d3cold.desc: Walks the GPU's PCI device + every upstream bridge up to the host. A PCIe endpoint can only enter D3cold if every link has d3cold_allowed=1 and power/control=auto. One pinned link silently leaves the slot in D3hot at idle — wastes ~10-30 W on a discrete GPU.
integrations.psiirq.title: 🌀 PSI irq + cpu.full
integrations.psiirq.desc: Reads /proc/pressure/irq (kernel ≥5.13) and the 'full' row from /proc/pressure/cpu. A wedged hardirq path (NIC MSI storm, NVMe interrupt loop) keeps system load looking idle while one CPU drowns — irq PSI is the canary that existing psi_pressure_audit misses.
integrations.fsquota.title: 📊 Filesystem quota / projid
integrations.fsquota.desc: Walks /proc/mounts for quota mount options + /proc/self/mountinfo for overlay upperdir resolution + /etc/projects orphan state. Catches the silent EDQUOT trap when a container layer upperdir lives on a prjquota'd FS, or quotas enabled with no userspace tool / project file.
integrations.fuse.title: 🔌 FUSE connections
integrations.fuse.desc: Walks /sys/fs/fuse/connections/<id>/{waiting, max_background, congestion_threshold}. Detects wedged userspace FUSE servers (sshfs / gvfs / mtpfs / AppImage / Flatpak portal helpers) leaving connections with waiting>0, plus orphan-mount accumulation.
integrations.keyringlife.title: 🔑 Keyring lifecycle
integrations.keyringlife.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/keys/{gc_delay, persistent_keyring_expiry} + /proc/keys + /etc/passwd. Detects keyrings owned by uids leaked from containers, persistent keyrings with disabled TTL, and absurd GC delays — adjacent to existing keyring_audit's quota check.
integrations.umwait.title: ⏱️ Intel umwait C0.2 + max_time
integrations.umwait.desc: Reads /sys/devices/system/cpu/umwait_control/{enable_c02, max_time} + /proc/cpuinfo waitpkg flag. With C0.2 enabled and the default max_time=100000, userspace spin loops (libuv, llama.cpp, Wayland compositor) park the core in a deep sub-state and pay 5-20 µs wake latency — input/audio stutter on latency-sensitive desktops.
integrations.splitlock.title: 🔒 Intel split-lock detection
integrations.splitlock.desc: Atomic ops crossing a cache-line cost ~1000x normal. Kernel mode set via cmdline split_lock_detect=. Wine/Proton games + JVM + V8 + BLAS emit them. fatal SIGBUS-kills offenders; off silently bleeds 10-15% perf.
integrations.oompol.title: 🧨 OOM policy sysctls
integrations.oompol.desc: Three sysctls turn a single runaway process into either a clean reclaim or a desktop reboot: vm.panic_on_oom, vm.oom_kill_allocating_task, vm.oom_dump_tasks. Adjacent to oom_priority (oom_score_adj) and panic_policy (kernel.panic) but distinct.
integrations.rseq.title: 🧵 rseq + futex_pi kernel posture
integrations.rseq.desc: glibc 2.35+ uses restartable sequences (rseq) for fast per-CPU malloc. CONFIG_RSEQ=n forces glibc into slower locking (~15-30% overhead on threaded LLM launchers). CONFIG_FUTEX_PI=n breaks RT audio priority-inheritance mutexes.
integrations.wqpe.title: ⚡ workqueue power_efficient
integrations.wqpe.desc: Reads /sys/module/workqueue/parameters/{power_efficient, cpu_intensive_thresh_us}. When power_efficient=Y leaks from a laptop default onto a desktop class machine, unbound WQs collapse onto a tiny power-efficient CPU subset — tens of ms tail latency on GPU submit / dm-md / NVMe paths.
integrations.bql.title: 🚦 BQL stall counters
integrations.bql.desc: Walks /sys/class/net/<dev>/queues/tx-*/byte_queue_limits/{stall_cnt, stall_max, stall_thrs}. The only kernel-visible signal for driver-side TX hangs that briefly wedge the link — invisible to softnet/ethtool.
integrations.perfsample.title: 📈 perf sampling limits
integrations.perfsample.desc: Reads kernel.perf_{cpu_time_max_percent, event_max_sample_rate, event_mlock_kb, event_max_stack}. Determines whether flamegraph profiling silently down-throttles, EPERMs ring-buffer mmap, or truncates stacks. Distinct from perf_event_paranoid.
integrations.zswapdeep.title: 🗜️ zswap deep-pool knobs
integrations.zswapdeep.desc: Reads /sys/module/zswap/parameters/{exclusive_loads, shrinker_enabled} + /sys/kernel/debug/zswap counters. Adjacent to zswap_zram_audit which covers basic enabled/compressor/pool-percent. exclusive_loads=N doubles RAM pressure invisibly.
integrations.kfence.title: 🧰 KFENCE memory-safety canary
integrations.kfence.desc: Reads /sys/module/kfence/parameters/sample_interval + CONFIG_KFENCE_SAMPLE_INTERVAL. KFENCE catches use-after-free / slab corruption inside the kernel — invaluable on hosts loading nvidia.ko since closed-driver regressions tend to surface as silent slab hits days before they panic.
integrations.qdisc.title: 🛣️ Default net qdisc
integrations.qdisc.desc: Reads /proc/sys/net/core/{default_qdisc, netdev_budget}. pfifo_fast has no fairness — Steam / HF model pulls bufferbloat the link. fq_codel / cake / fq is the desktop-correct default.
integrations.fscache.title: 💾 FS-Cache + cachefiles
integrations.fscache.desc: Walks /proc/fs/fscache/caches + /proc/mounts for NFS 'fsc' option + /sys/fs/cachefiles. Catches the trap where an NFS-exported model checkpoint dir is mounted with fsc but cachefilesd isn't running — 20+ GB cold-path re-downloads.
integrations.ksmadv.title: 🪞 KSM advisor + smart_scan
integrations.ksmadv.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/mm/ksm/{advisor_mode, smart_scan, advisor_target_scan_time}. KSM running with advisor_mode=none + smart_scan=0 burns 5-15% of one CPU walking every anonymous page for nothing. Adjacent to existing ksm_audit.
integrations.uncore.title: 🧮 Intel uncore (LLC) freq
integrations.uncore.desc: Walks /sys/devices/system/cpu/intel_uncore_frequency/package_*_die_*/{min,max,current,initial_min,initial_max}_freq_khz. Uncore (LLC/ring/mesh) freq is independent of core P-states; firmware clamps cap GPU↔CPU LLC bandwidth during inference.
integrations.modprobedrift.title: 🚫 modprobe blacklist drift
integrations.modprobedrift.desc: Parses /etc/modprobe.d, /run/modprobe.d, /usr/lib/modprobe.d, /lib/modprobe.d for blacklist / install /bin/true directives and cross-checks /proc/modules. Classic homelab footgun: blacklist nouveau but forget update-initramfs → reset storms.
integrations.sigenforce.title: 🔐 module sig_enforce vs SB
integrations.sigenforce.desc: Reads /sys/module/module/parameters/sig_enforce + /sys/kernel/security/lockdown + /sys/firmware/efi/efivars/SecureBoot. Common Secure-Boot mismatch: SB enabled in firmware but sig_enforce=N → modules load unsigned despite SB.
integrations.bpfharden.title: 🛡️ BPF JIT hardening
integrations.bpfharden.desc: Reads net.core.bpf_jit_{harden, kallsyms, limit}. bpf_jit_harden=0 + unprivileged_bpf=0 leaves the JIT spectre-vulnerable for unpriv users; bpf_jit_kallsyms=1 leaks JIT symbols into /proc/kallsyms weakening KASLR.
integrations.oopswarn.title: 🚨 kernel oops/warn counters
integrations.oopswarn.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/oops_count + /sys/kernel/warn_count (kernel 6.2+) — the cheapest 'did the kernel silently scream overnight' signal. No dmesg scrape, no root, no journald query.
integrations.ephport.title: 🔢 Ephemeral TCP port window
integrations.ephport.desc: Reads net.ipv4.ip_local_port_range + ip_unprivileged_port_start + /proc/net/tcp{,6} usage. Long-lived homelab boxes eventually run out of source ports — connect() then returns EADDRNOTAVAIL.
integrations.zramwb.title: 🗜️ zram writeback + recompress
integrations.zramwb.desc: Reads /sys/block/zram*/{backing_dev, recomp_algorithm, mm_stat, bd_stat}. Kernel 6.3+ adds idle-page eviction + secondary compression — on heavy-swap desktops cold pages should drift from zram to a backing device.
integrations.uclamp.title: 🎚️ cgroup v2 uclamp + zswap
integrations.uclamp.desc: Walks /sys/fs/cgroup/{system.slice, user.slice}/cpu.uclamp.* + memory.zswap.*. Failure mode: a game launcher accidentally sets cpu.uclamp.max=50 on user.slice and GPU-feeder threads run at half speed.
integrations.hwpboost.title: 🚀 HWP dynamic_boost
integrations.hwpboost.desc: Reads /sys/devices/system/cpu/intel_pstate/hwp_dynamic_boost — a separate burst-bias toggle from EPP / no_turbo. Active HWP with boost=0 loses 5-15 % short-burst responsiveness on gaming / Wayland / input.
integrations.hungtask.title: ⏳ hung_task warnings drift
integrations.hungtask.desc: Reads kernel.hung_task_{warnings, check_interval_secs, timeout_secs}. The warnings counter decrements toward 0 — once exhausted, new D-state hangs are silent. panic_policy enumerates it but doesn't classify.
integrations.fwloader.title: 📦 firmware loader policy
integrations.fwloader.desc: Reads /sys/class/firmware/timeout + /sys/kernel/firmware_config/{force,ignore}_sysfs_fallback. Slow USB cameras / Wi-Fi cards / FPGA blobs time out at cold boot when timeout is too short or fallback is blocked.
integrations.imafresh.title: 📑 IMA log freshness
integrations.imafresh.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/security/ima/ascii_runtime_measurements (the log itself) + count, checks for boot_aggregate PCR10 anchor. ima_integrity_audit reads counters only, never the log content.
integrations.imadigest.title: 🔏 IMA digest_lists
integrations.imadigest.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/security/integrity/{digest_lists_loaded, digest_lists/}. IMA-appraisal enforcement (Secure-Boot homelab) needs digest lists pre-loaded; enforce + zero lists = every exec denied. ima_integrity_audit covers measurement counters only.
integrations.drmgt.title: 🎮 DRM GT firmware (i915/amdgpu)
integrations.drmgt.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/debug/dri/<N>/gt/uc/{guc_info, huc_info} + amdgpu/gpu_recovery. Intel GuC failure breaks compute/level-zero silently without bumping reset_count; HuC failure kills HEVC/AV1 decode.
integrations.pmasync.title: 💤 /sys/power suspend tunables
integrations.pmasync.desc: Reads /sys/power/{pm_async, pm_freeze_timeout, sync_on_suspend, pm_print_times}. Governs resume latency and dirty-data safety; sync_on_suspend=0 loses unwritten pages on power-loss-during-suspend.
integrations.vmcompact.title: 🗜️ Proactive compaction
integrations.vmcompact.desc: Reads vm.compaction_proactiveness + compact_unevictable_allowed + percpu_pagelist_high_fraction. Proactiveness=0 with THP=always forces khugepaged to do all compaction synchronously — model load stalls.
integrations.iodelay.title: 🐌 x86 io_delay_type
integrations.iodelay.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/io_delay_type. Modes 0/1/2/3 = outb 0x80 / udelay / 0xed / none. Mode 3 risky on consumer boards (SMBus/EC drivers); mode 0 adds ~1 µs/op on modern x86 — ACPI path latency.
integrations.printkpace.title: ⏸️ printk pacing
integrations.printkpace.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/{printk_delay, printk_devkmsg, printk_ratelimit_burst}. printk_delay > 0 wedges the logger CPU for ms each line — a noisy driver (nvidia init, nvme reset) can hang seconds.
integrations.l2cache.title: 📦 L2 cache imbalance
integrations.l2cache.desc: Walks /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/cache/index2/size. Hybrid Intel Alder/Raptor/Meteor and AMD Phoenix-Z ship P-cores and E-cores with different L2. Pin LLM threads to the larger-L2 cluster.
integrations.setspeed.title: 🎯 cpufreq setspeed drift
integrations.setspeed.desc: Reads /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu*/cpufreq/{scaling_setspeed, scaling_governor}. The 'userspace' governor pins a freq via setspeed; stale undervolt/thermal-test scripts often leave it pinned far below max.
integrations.vmnuma.title: 🗺️ vm.numa_stat + zonelist
integrations.vmnuma.desc: Reads /proc/sys/vm/{numa_stat, numa_zonelist_order} + /sys/devices/system/node/online. On multi-node hosts, numa_stat=0 kills per-node counters; zonelist_order=Node defeats zone fallback.
integrations.sysrqcad.title: ⌨️ Ctrl-Alt-Del + poweroff_cmd
integrations.sysrqcad.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/{ctrl-alt-del, poweroff_cmd}. CAD=1 triggers immediate hard reboot (skips userspace shutdown); custom poweroff_cmd path runs at shutdown and needs audit.
integrations.dirtybytes.title: 💾 vm.dirty_bytes drift
integrations.dirtybytes.desc: Reads /proc/sys/vm/{dirty_bytes, dirty_background_bytes, dirty_ratio, dirty_background_ratio}. Kernel uses bytes when non-zero, silently overriding ratio. Operators reading the ratio get a false sense of the limit.
integrations.numascan.title: 🧮 NUMA balancing scan tuning
integrations.numascan.desc: Reads /proc/sys/kernel/numa_balancing_scan_{delay, period_min, period_max, size}_*. Mis-tuning hurts large-RSS LLMs — tiny period_min adds fault overhead; huge period_max stalls promotion on tiered/CXL memory.
integrations.nvdrm.title: 🖥️ nvidia_drm KMS posture
integrations.nvdrm.desc: Reads /sys/module/nvidia_drm/parameters/{modeset, fbdev}. Distinct from RM-side /sys/module/nvidia/parameters/* (covered by kmod_params). modeset=N is the most common silent Wayland failure on homelab desktops.
integrations.overlay.title: 📚 overlayfs driver tunables
integrations.overlay.desc: Reads /sys/module/overlay/parameters/{metacopy, redirect_dir, xino_auto}. Container/snap workflows depend on these — redirect_dir=N breaks rename-on-directory; metacopy=N makes chmod on large files copy the whole file.
integrations.dmmod.title: 🧱 dm_mod driver posture
integrations.dmmod.desc: Reads /sys/module/dm_mod/parameters/{use_blk_mq, dm_numa_node}. use_blk_mq=N is the legacy request-based path (slower); dm_numa_node != -1 pins dm threads to a specific NUMA node.
integrations.cgtree.title: 🌳 cgroup tree limits
integrations.cgtree.desc: Reads /sys/fs/cgroup/{cgroup.max.depth, cgroup.max.descendants, cgroup.stat}. Surfaces runaway cgroup creation (runc leak, systemd-userdb spam) by checking nr_descendants/max ratio; cgroup_root_audit reads these but never classifies.
integrations.numademo.title: ↘️ NUMA demotion_enabled
integrations.numademo.desc: Reads /sys/kernel/mm/numa/demotion_enabled (kernel 5.15+) + /sys/devices/system/node/online. Single boolean controlling whether cold pages demote to a slower NUMA tier (CXL.mem, PMEM, far DDR). Weakest pick: only fires on multi-node tiered-memory hosts.
integrations.acpiboot.title: 🎨 ACPI BGRT + FPDT
integrations.acpiboot.desc: Reads /sys/firmware/acpi/{bgrt, fpdt}. BGRT.status bit 0 indicates valid boot-logo display; FPDT is firmware boot-time decomposition (absent on most VMs/desktops). acpi_audit covers other ACPI surfaces but never bgrt/fpdt.
integrations.acpitbl.title: 📜 ACPI table set sanity
integrations.acpitbl.desc: Walks /sys/firmware/acpi/tables/. Flags missing SRAT on multi-node hosts, huge DSDT (vendor BIOS bloat), excess SSDTs (firmware fragmentation). No existing module enumerates this directory.
integrations.pcinuma.title: 🔗 PCI NUMA pinning
integrations.pcinuma.desc: Fleet-walks /sys/bus/pci/devices/*/numa_node. On multi-node hosts where every PCI device shows numa_node=-1, ACPI _PXM is missing for slots — hot-path NIC/NVMe will cross-traverse for memory.
integrations.swappri.title: 🔀 swap priority tiering
integrations.swappri.desc: Parses /proc/swaps Priority column. Real foot-gun: disk swap with higher priority than zram bypasses zram entirely. swap_tunables_audit reads /proc/swaps but never parses Priority.
integrations.xfslog.title: 🗃️ XFS log activity
integrations.xfslog.desc: Walks /sys/fs/xfs/<dev>/stats/stats. fs_specific_tunables_audit reads the corruption regex only — the broader stats blob (rw row + log counters) is otherwise unparsed. Real value on XFS NAS / Steam library hosts.
integrations.collprof.title: ⏱ Fleet collection profile
integrations.collprof.desc: Self-observability: times every module's status() call and surfaces the top-10 slowest. Lazy-load only — collection takes ~8 s on a full fleet. EXPECTED_SLOW modules are tagged [expected] and excluded from the per-module budget.
integrations.pcierec.title: PCIe Recovery Wizard
integrations.pcierec.desc: Diagnose GPU link / driver stuck state and walk through escalating recovery steps. VM-aware: surfaces host-side Proxmox commands when running under KVM.
integrations.pcierec.wrapper_install_hel: Run this command in a terminal on the host, then re-check:
integrations.pcierec.wrapper_ready: Recovery executable from the OcuLink card (🔧 Recover link button).

link_stable.tooltip: Locks GPU clocks so the PCIe link stays at its highest sustainable speed (usually Gen 2 on cheap Aliexpress OcuLink docks instead of flapping). Cost: ~+13 W idle.
link_stable.gen_4_hint: Heavy lock 2000+ MHz — rejected by cheap retimers
alerts.detect_tooltip: Send any message to your bot first (e.g. /start), then click here to auto-fill your chat_id
alerts.detect_no_msg: No messages received by the bot — send /start to your bot in Telegram and retry
alerts.botfather_tooltip: Open @BotFather in Telegram to create a new bot (command /newbot)
installer.pcie_recovery_wrapper.label: PCIe Recovery wrapper
installer.pcie_recovery_wrapper.descript: Sudoers wrapper for the PCIe recovery wizard. Allows execute-mode recovery from the OcuLink card.
installer.oculink_watchdog.label: OcuLink watchdog daemon
installer.oculink_watchdog.description: systemd service that polls the GPU link and logs drops/recoveries. Source of truth for the OcuLink card's history.
installer.power_limit_wrapper.label: Power limit wrapper
installer.power_limit_wrapper.descriptio: Sudoers wrapper for `nvidia-smi -pl`. Required by the power_limit module's live TDP slider.
installer.coolbits_xorg.label: Xorg CoolBits
installer.coolbits_xorg.description: Adds the CoolBits option to xorg.conf so nvidia-settings can drive fan curves and clock offsets.
installer.link_stable_wrapper.label: Link Stable Mode wrapper
installer.link_stable_wrapper.descriptio: Sudoers wrapper that lets the dashboard clock-lock the GPU. Used to keep the PCIe link at sustained Gen 2 instead of flapping Gen 1 / Gen 4 on cheap OcuLink retimers.
installer.witness_dpkg_hook.label: Witness — auto-snapshot on apt
installer.witness_dpkg_hook.description: Installs an apt dpkg hook that takes a Witness snapshot before AND after every apt operation. Gives you a guaranteed 'before upgrade' baseline to diff against if tok/s tanks after the next reboot.
shadow.tooltip: Cross-check nvidia-smi (sampled) with a Shelly wall meter and DS18B20 probe. Catches PSU losses, fan power, and the peaks NVML misses.
shadow.empty_help: Add SHADOW_SHELLY_URL=http://… and/or SHADOW_W1_DEVICE=auto in config.env.
witness.tooltip: Snapshot of driver, modules, packages, and PCIe sysfs. Diff two snapshots to bisect a tok/s regression after an update.
witness.empty: No snapshots yet. Take one before every driver/kernel upgrade.
witness.no_changes: No differences detected. The system is identical.

llm.tooltip_lifetime: Lifetime total since first install — cumulates across restarts

apptriggers.hint: When a listed process is running, auto-profile forces the chosen profile (overrides load-based classification).
apptriggers.examples: Examples : blender·llama-server·cyberpunk·steam-runtime. Match is case-insensitive substring against /proc/<pid>/comm.
benchmark.title: Profile comparison benchmark
benchmark.hint: Run a workload on profile A then B for the given duration, then compare power, throughput, and efficiency.
benchmark.step1: Pick 2 profiles to compare (e.g. silent vs boost)
benchmark.step2: Click Run — the daemon applies profile A, waits the chosen duration, then profile B
benchmark.step3: The table below shows averaged metrics per profile + which one 'wins' per criterion
benchmark.workload_hint: For useful results, run your typical workload (training, gaming, llama-bench) BEFORE pressing ▶ and keep it active during both segments.
benchmark.result_note: Values are averaged over each segment's window. The winning side per row is highlighted.
